Print a shell completion script for the given shell

Usage:
  nectar completions [--json] <shell>

Arguments:
  shell               Target shell: bash, zsh, fish, or powershell

Options:
  -j, --json          No-op for `completions`; accepted so the global `--json` flag does not error here. The output is always shell-script text
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > config > default).
  Naming: NECTAR_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --json → NECTAR_COMPLETIONS_JSON

Examples:
  nectar completions bash >> ~/.bashrc
  nectar completions zsh > ~/.zsh/_nectar
  nectar completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/nectar.fish
